observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate in the immediate implementation of the projects, programs and activities under the        20% Development Fund. Likewise, we recommend that management implement programs/projects/activities on the scheduled dates in a given year in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.2017-1 in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and desired outcomes from the said programs/projects.                        (Part II, Finding No. 1)
We recommended that the management adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG (JMC) No. 2020-1 dated November 4, 2020 in order to ensure effective and proper utilization of the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the unreconciled difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same balances in the individual financial statements. Likewise, we recommend that henceforth, management make an immediate review and conduct tracing of the differences between the two accounts once periodic financial statements are generated so as to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all concerned officers and employees, requiring immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, we recommend the withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government. Likewise, it is recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipient of funds transferred from the municipality, in order to fairly present the account Due from Local Government Units in the financial statements and provide proof that the purpose for the fund transfer has already been attained. Also, refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommended that management implement the programs/projects/ activities funded under the continuing appropriations pursuant to the intents of Section 305(l) 306(e) of R.A.7160 in order to promptly/aptly deliver the intended benefits due the general public. Also, we recommend that Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Engineer reconcile their records in order to arrive at same project accomplishment and timely completion.

We recommended that management draw the following journal entry to adjust the account Due to LGUs and recognize items to be properly recorded under the various liability accounts:
Due to LGUs					   ₱221,609.39		      Due to Other National Government Agencies		₱120,993.12
Due to Other Fund				                23,971.44
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable			    69,644.83
Trust Liabilities					      7,000.00
Also, we recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to review those items which are of doubtful nature and coordinate with the concerned department head as to the status of implementation of fund transfers, when applicable, so as to make the necessary adjustments in order to fairly present the balance of the account Due to LGUs in the financial statements.

We recommended that management consider adopting the procedures in reconciling and cleaning the dormant receivable and liability accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers per COA Circular No.2016-009. Also, we recommend that management send confirmation letters to its creditors such as BIR, PAGIBIG, Philhealth and GSIS to validate outstanding payables. Confirmation letters and adjusting journal entry vouchers, if any, shall be furnished the audit team.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of Section 54.3 of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A.9184 on the posting requirement of notice of award, contract or purchase order in the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity, in order to ensure transparency in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management discontinue granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than those stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, in order to ensure proper segregation of duties between custody of fund and authorization on the granting of cash advances and establish full accountability for public funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	Submit a written justification for granting cash advances in the procurement of supplies directly related to the mitigation and containment of the transmission of COVID-19 instead of Negotiated Procurement (Emergency Cases); b. Regulate the granting of cash advances only for purposes in accordance with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No.97-002; c. Adhere to the relevant provisions of RA 9184 in the procurement of supplies, especially COA-GPPB JMC No.1, when applying the Negotiated Procurement (Emergency Cases) in addressing urgent procurement concerns of the municipality.

B)  Various supplies totaling ₱258,777.49 purchased out of the ₱300,000.00 cash advance were not aligned to the specific legal purpose for which it was granted thus, defeating the purposes of DBM LBC No.124 in the effective utilization of COVID-19 fund which is to provide basic services to constituents affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended that management direct the disbursing/accountable officer to use the cash advance solely for the legal specific purpose for which it was granted, to align with the objectives of DBM LBC No.124 in the effective utilization of COVID-19 fund, in order to ensure delivery of basic services to constituents affected by the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management set aside funds for the installation of the prescribed e-NGAS both in the accounting and budget departments and henceforth, make use of the system in the submission of pertinent financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Management said that they will set aside budget for the implementation of e-NGAS and will comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The accounting department still uses their current system while the budget department uses ECPAC system.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management revise/update its Revenue Code of 2009 pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 to increase the revenue collection of the municipality so as to provide additional funds which can be used to finance beneficial programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: Management said that a review of the Local Revenue Code was scheduled mid last year.
observations.reason: Review of the Local Revenue Code was not conducted because management failed to make a schedule with BLGF because of the lockdown.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exhaust all possible remedies to collect these receivables, deduct the outstanding balances from the salaries or any amount due from the employees who are still connected with the municipality, if any and review, analyze and reconcile all overdue accounts to determine proper disposition of reconciled and validated accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management said that these receivables were outstanding prior to CY 2013. Management sees the collection possibility as rare to none. They are also contemplating to request for write off of these accounts from the books on the premise that collection is very remote. However, due to lacking documentation, accounting is at a limitation to even break down the accounts.
observations.reason: The receivable accounts still remain in the books as of to date. And management has not requested a write-off for these accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit all the original copies of the required documents to avoid the issuance of notice of suspension.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant properly review the supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers to ensure that these consist of all the necessary documents required to be submitted especially the As Built Plan and complete set of Approved Plans/Drawings.
observations.managementAction: The management said that as for the plans, drawings and designs, these were prepared in limited quantity and sometimes fall short of the number of agencies requiring or seeking a copy of such documents. However, the engineering office will comply and submit the required documents as well as the accounting office will ensure that copies of these documents will be filed by the agency before payment.
observations.reason: Management fails to submit the lacking supporting documents as of present.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management revisit the current regular plantilla items in order to avoid the practice of allowing contract of service and job order workers to perform the duties and responsibilities of a regular employee. Likewise, we recommend that management assign the collection of water bills to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office where there are bonded regular employees.
observations.managementAction: Management said that they will discuss and adhere to the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Management still has contract of service who performs the duties and responsibilities of a regular employee.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive secure and maintain with responsible insurers such insurances for these completed projects to cover risk of fire, flood and other damages in accordance with the Tripartite Memorandum of Agreement for the DILG-BEA Fund to minimize risk of loss of funds and depletion of financial resources.
observations.managementAction: Management said that they will coordinate with GSIS to determine the possible insurance cost for the properties mentioned. And that the cost of insurance be included in the current DRRM plan.
observations.reason: Management has not secured an insurance coverage for the completed projects to cover risk of fire, flood and other damages in accordance with the Tripartite Memorandum of Agreement for the DILG-BEA Fund to minimize risk of loss of funds and depletion of financial resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to examine the Other Payables account, prepare necessary adjustments in the books and submit to COA the corresponding copies of the adjusting entries and its supporting documents, if any, for verification.
observations.managementAction: Management is looking for supporting documents in order to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: As per financial reports submitted, no adjusting entry has been made as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the local government unit return the unutilized PDAF balance to the Bureau of Treasury for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the national government in accordance with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Received/Signed copy of the liquidation report is still to be recovered to expedite the return of the balance to the PTO-Bohol.
observations.reason: Until present, PDAF received is still recorded in the books and were not returned by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.
observations.managementAction: Management has not complied with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: No legal basis is submitted by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit copies of the perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/issuance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review of contracts and purchase orders and to avoid imposition of administrative disciplinary action prescribed by laws.
observations.managementAction: Management has not complied with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: As of present, copies of the perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and/or officials responsible for or in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items regardless of amount shall, within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance, furnish the Auditor with copies of the delivery receipts and inspection and acceptance report and notify the said Auditor of the deliveries in order to enable to prepare for and perform timely inspection of the same.
observations.managementAction: Management has not complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Management still fails to furnish the auditor with copies of the delivery receipts and inspection and acceptance report within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit all the original copies of the necessary supporting documents as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 and observe the provisions of the said circular on the submission of copy of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents and of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445. 

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant properly review the supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers to ensure that these consist of all the necessary documents required to be submitted and that these are correctly and completely filled up to serve as valid documents in support of the transactions involving financial claims.
observations.managementAction: Management is going to locate the original copies of the necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: Supporting documents are still not forwarded to the office of the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management pay directly to the suppliers through issuance of checks in the procurement of supplies pursuant to COA Circular                No.97-002 and pertinent provisions of R.A.9184.
observations.managementAction: Management believes that it is beneficial to pursue the current action since food and supplies used for supplementing feeding are bought from different vendors and mostly in small quantities only.
observations.reason: Until present, cash advances are still resorted by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

(a) Require the Municipal Accountant and other concern offices to keep accounts and support transaction in such details/complete information to meet the needs of the agency/office especially to facilitate the tracing of the transactions as well as in the monitoring, control and analysis thereof.

(b) Require the concern officers to formulate collection strategy so to reduce or eliminate outstanding receivables particularly from officers and employees

(c ) Require the Municipal and other concern officers/employees to retrieve information to support and completely document a transaction or an account.
observations.managementAction: Management believes that these accounts have a remote to no possibility of collection due to the time that these accounts are collectible.  Management will request the Commission that these accounts be written off.
observations.reason: As of to date, no request for write off has been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the required documents such as (1) brief summary of the reported program or project; (2) abstract of reported studies; (3) copies of reported policy issuances; and (40 curriculum vitae of trainers or consultants of the reported training/s. Agencies shall submit to the DBM two (2) copies of the annual GAD accomplishment report for the previous year along with the agency budget proposal in accordance with the budget call in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2004-01 of the Department of Budget and Management, the National Economic and Development Authority and the National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women and the 2005 Updated Budget Operations Manual for LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management has not complied with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Management failed to submit the enumerated document and failed to identify the specific projects to be funded by GAD fund.

observations.recommendation:
Secure a copy of the contract/s and all its supporting documents pertaining to the P1.8 million loan from the LBP and furnish the COA for post-audit purposes.

Require the Municipal Accountant to give a proper and complete accounting (computational analysis of the loan transactions and her basis of taking up) of the Loans Receivable account including the payments received from the Cooperative’s members for the financial assistance granted.

Require the Municipal Accountant to take up the whole amount of financial assistance granted to the Cooperative as loan to include the interest and other bank charges arising from the loan.  Officials and members of the association should be held accountable on the funds received. 

Require the submission of lacking supporting documents and enforce liquidation of the financial assistance from the SRCPMPC to avoid suspensions/disallowance in audit.

Conduct review and evaluation of the whole transaction, determine the persons responsible for the consummation of the whole transaction, and if warranted, initiate the filing of appropriate charges against local officials concerned for the violations committed;

Require refunds from persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Management has not complied with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Documents are not yet retrieved as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and provide the composition of the accounts with markings “For ID”. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary working papers to validate the reliability and existence of the account balances.
Require the Inventory Committee to properly plan and execute the physical inventory taking, with consideration to safety and health protocols, so as to be able to prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of PPE.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management send a final demand to accountable officers and immediately withhold the unliquidated amounts from their salaries after the lapse of the grace period as indicated in the demand letter, pursuant to Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management discontinue granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than those stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, in order to ensure proper segregation of duties between custody of fund and authorization on the granting of cash advances and establish full accountability for public funds.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the disallowed amount from the salaries of person or other compensation due the person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance or charge so as to restitute the local government unit of the much needed fund to be used for its daily operations or other worthwhile projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management:
Adopt the perpetual inventory method for the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicine Supplies and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies by setting up Inventory Accounts.
Coordinate directly with the concerned departments for the submission of duly acknowledged Requisition and Issue Slips for the withdrawal/issuance of supplies and the Monthly Report of the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicines, and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies issued and its supporting documents, in order to establish inventories on hand at year-end.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the persons who will prepare the purchase requests, request for quotations, purchase orders, and inspection and acceptance report to provide narrative description, size/dimension/volume, model, classification and other necessary descriptions of supplies to be procured so as to guarantee delivery of goods which meets the effectiveness or performance requirements of the end-user. Also, we recommended that Management submit a written explanation for the lapse in providing item descriptions on various purchases.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to require the submission of warranty securities from the suppliers and for all its future procurement of goods.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management, especially the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, immediately work on securing the necessary documents as required by COA Region Technical Services Office so as to establish the propriety and validity of these infrastructure contracts. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, properly review the supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers to ensure that these consist of all the necessary documents required to be submitted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the BAC to demand the submission of Class “A” documents from Rhoy Stainless Welding Shop and submit the same to the resident auditor. We also recommend that the BAC, during the post qualification of bidders, review the complete of all required documents in order to determine whether they are eligible to participate in the procurement of items.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management pay suppliers and contractors thru check in so as to withhold and immediately remit taxes due to the BIR, per Section 1 of RA No, 1051 and Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 5-2006.

We recommend that management be more prudent in the disbursement of funds to ensure sound fiscal administration pursuant to the provisions of Section 305 and 337 of R.A.7160 and management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record and apportion correctly the share from internal revenue collections so that charges/expenditures thereof can be aptly allotted or regulated.
observations.managementAction:
Management said that the reason for the net loss is primarily due to not achieving the targeted collections. As the agency relies heavily on the collections from local sources, not meeting those collections would lead to a net loss since the approved annual appropriation is based on those estimated collections.

Also, management has already anticipated the impact of non-achieving targeted collections and has made endeavors to properly and scientifically forecast the estimated collections for CY 2020.
observations.reason: Based on the financial statements submitted, the municipality did not incur a loss for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management explain the recognition of obligations without the corresponding cash back-up and how these obligations will be met. Henceforth, we also recommend that management monitor cash utilization and refrain from using cash balances intended for trust funds, liabilities and reserve to avoid penalties, surcharges and legal implications in case the municipality would be unable to meet obligations.
observations.managementAction: Management said that the reason for the negative cash balance is primarily due to not achieving the targeted collections. Also, management said that they will exert more effort in order to replenish the cash deficit.
observations.reason: As of                          December 31, 2020, the municipality has sufficient cash backup as per verification of the financial reports submitted.

observations.managementAction:
Management said that the electronic system currently in use does not allow for actual time to be inputted rather only for approved leave and holidays. All DTRs though printed individually were verified by HRMO for attendance logs.

In as much as management would comply with the audit recommendation, however, photocopying the logbook to be attached to a claim would be redundant and costly to the agency. The management believes that the control in place is sufficient to verify the validity of the employees’ attendance log. Moreover, management is implementing a strict policy on attendance logs. Failure to log for whatever reason other than approved leave of absence, on official business, on errand or on electrical failures will be considered tardy or absent.
observations.reason: Management already required all municipal employees to log in and out in the official logbook and not just in the biometrics. Hence, in case the biometrics was not able to capture the time ins and outs of an employee, the logbook will be the basis of the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit, if there is any, the required approval/authorization from the sanggunian before the implementation of the projects. And in view of the Supreme Court’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that all appropriations shall be incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan and Project Procurement Management Plan of the LGU and implemented by the concerned department or office.
observations.managementAction: Management said that the generic items mentioned for projects funded under the 20% Development Fund have already been clarified under SB Resolution No. 2019-58. Also some of the have been re-aligned per SB Resolution No. 2019-185, of which and in compliance to AOM 2019-017(18) are not in generic terms.
observations.reason: A separate SB resolution has been issued for projects described in generic terms.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the contractor to submit document/s to support the claim that the delivery of HDPE Pipes from the recommended manufacturer will consume almost the entire contract duration and proof that the suspension advised by the Municipality Mayor caused delay in the excavation for the pipe laying to avoid liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a written explanation on the said delay.
observations.reason: Management already submitted the documents and is pending review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). 

We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: Management already sent a request for approval to BLGF and awaiting reply.
observations.reason:
The Bureau of Local Government Finance issued Memorandum Circular No. 07-01-2017 dated July 3, 2017 re: DOF Department Circular No. 01.2017 (Amended Guidelines on Authorized Government Depositary Banks) which prescribed the maximum maintaining balance of LGUs with non-AGDBs. As per schedule, fourth class municipalities like Municipality of Sagbayan may maintain a maximum balance of ₱5,500,000.00. 
As of December 31, 2020, the municipality has the following balances with non-AGDB:
GF–FCB: ₱4,978,203.99
SEF-FCB: ₱636,003.31
TF-FCB: ₱997,112.74

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the department heads to timely and properly plan the trainings, conventions and seminar/workshops in a year so as identify charges to the account Training Expenses. We also recommend that management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Account to properly charge and record expenditures to its appropriate expense accounts to avoid interchangeable use thereof. Likewise, we recommend that management encourage department heads to defray petty food expenses out of their Representation Allowance, especially for food accommodation of their own visitors/guests, considering that the purpose of which is to expedite food payment without the need of going through the tedious process of preparing the vouchers and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Management said that these transactions have been erroneously recorded in the Representation Expense account. To ensure that this will not happen again in the future, they will place measures especially for bookkeepers to properly identify when to book transactions under Representation and Training Expenses.
observations.reason: Trainings, conventions and seminar/workshops are no longer booked under Representation Expense.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit justification in writing on why financial assistance under the AICS Program were granted to clients despite the absence of supporting documents as enumerated in Section 4.2 of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15, Series of 2014.  

Also, direct the Municipal Social Welfare Officer to submit the records of the cases and all other basic supporting documents relative to the claims for further evaluation, otherwise the transactions will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted justification and is now attaching the necessary documents as required.
observations.reason: Financial assistance under AICS program are now supported with pertinent documents as required in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15 dated June 9, 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare the Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality to comply with the requirements of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and implement the same.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared the Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality to comply with the requirements of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.reason: Management already has a Solid Waste Management Plan for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP meeting.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction: Management wrote a letter to the VMLP with regards to this audit finding.
observations.reason: No justification submitted for using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts. However, the Municipal Treasurer no longer accepts old receipts issued by VMLP and PCL.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the contractors to submit an explanation for the delay of completion and to complete the project as provided in the terms and conditions specified in the contract executed by them, if still warranted.

Also, it is recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to compute and deduct from the contractor’s payment liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a written explanation on the said delay.
observations.reason: Management already submitted written explanation and other supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should see to it that Gender and Development (GAD) Plan of the agency will fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives towards the role of women in becoming a participative partner in nation building and development, with the end view of protecting their lives and rights and to live a modest life in a society.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that GAD projects are culled out from the general appropriation based on its merits.
observations.reason: As per review, GAD programs and activities of the municipality addressed and adhered to the basic purpose of GAD.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Require the municipal accountant to send billings and/or demand letters to the individuals concerned who are still in the locality or whose whereabouts are known for possible collections.

If collection is futile for valid reasons, request authority from the Commission on Audit for the write off of these accounts.

Require the municipal accountant that for whatever result in the request for write off of the accounts, take the necessary adjustment in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The management has already forwarded to the local auditor of the request for write-off. For the first recommendation, manpower and financial requirements cannot be allocated for these accounts which this management believes that collection is futile. 

Taking into account the time that these accounts have been dormant, there are no more records available that this management can use to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: Demand letters were already sent to the individuals concerned.

observations.recommendation:
Stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deduction from the payroll.

Terminate immediately the arrangement (MOA) with the FCB and with other private lending institutions.

For regular employees who wish to avail of such loan facility, they may transact directly with the private lending institution concerned without involving the LGU personnel and resources to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses.

Follow strictly the provisions of Section 262 of the GAAM, and the pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Payments of loan amortizations to private banks have been stopped from including in the payroll.  Management cannot terminate the arrangement with the private bank as the arrangement is not specific only to loans from employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal accountant to properly monitor and check payments to BIR so as to avoid double remittances. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace in the previous accounting records those balances in the “For Identification” account, in order to arrive at the correct Due to BIR account balance.
observations.managementAction: Management said that the negative balance was due to an error in the inclusion of a municipality’s counterpart for the activity. In effect, the counterpart for the Municipality of Trinidad was included twice when determining the total funds available for the activity.
observations.reason: Until present, no adjustments are made for the remaining ₱53,152.88. Out of this amount, ₱46,217.84 is classified as “For Identification” in which the accounting has no available data to be used for reconciliation. While the remaining are double remittances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit immediately the necessary supporting documents required by the Office of the COA Chairman and furnish us copies of the duly received transmittal letters for the liquidation of CY 2011 and 2015 unliquidated cash advances of the present mayor, as well as those of the former mayors, if already transmitted. 

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash advances if liquidation vouchers/reports for the previous cash advances are not yet submitted to the COA Chairman, duly supported by a received copy of the transmittal letter.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the transmittal letters of the recent cash advances for confidential/intelligence fund were already forwarded to the auditor. However, previous transmittal letters are still to be located especially those of previous mayors in which the present management has no copies.
observations.reason: Copies of the received transmittal letters are not forwarded to the auditor especially those former mayors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’ payable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management had already reconciled some of these balances, however, the rest are still to be reconciled due to lacking documents.
observations.reason: Prior year balances are still for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the MDRRMO, MSWDO and all Punong Barangays submit written justifications duly signed by all officials and personnel concerned and involved in the materials distribution, as to the non-observance of the terms of the MOA between the NHA and LGU-Sagbayan and likewise, furnish COA documents that would support their assertions as to the agreement between the constituents and Sangguniang Barangay which transpired before the actual distribution of materials.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted some the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Documents that would support Management’s assertions as to the agreement between the constituents and Sangguniang Barangay are still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and web administrator or its equivalent produce and regularly update official website for the Municipality to enhance transparency and accountability on financial transactions and operations thereof pursuant to Section 98 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2015.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of creating their official website.
observations.reason: As per interview, management already agreed to set aside funds to create their official website.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the local chief executive initiate the move by creating the inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all properties of the municipality.  The corresponding equipment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts.  In the absence of available records of acquisitions in the past years, they will have no other recourse but to take hold of the disbursement vouchers to obtain the necessary data to be recorded in the equipment/property ledger cards.

We further recommend that management require the general services officer or municipal treasurer to submit the request for relief of accountability with complete supporting papers so that the auditors can take proper action on the request.
observations.managementAction: Management had already conducted an inventory count, however, it is not yet complete. Hence, management is going to conduct a by department inventory count in order to finish completely and will submit the inventory report after.
observations.reason: Physical count is being conducted by Management as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

Verify the Memorandum of Understanding or Memorandum of Agreement entered into with the donor-agencies to ascertain on the disposition/treatment of the balances of the Financial Assistance received.  

Transfer the unexpended balances in accordance with Sections 98 and 99 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management said that MOAs/MOUs could not be retrieved for verification. And no claim has been made, administratively.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going, specifically for accounts 2013 and prior.

observations.recommendation:
Require the person/s who authorized the construction of the said building on a    private property to submit justification of his/their action or other appropriate document that ensured the protection of the interest of the LGU;

Negotiate with the owner of the private lot where the Corn Post-Harvest Facilities is located for the acquisition of the said property by the LGU through sale or expropriation as mandated under Section 19 of the Local Government Code or RA 7160.

Henceforth, submit to the COA all contracts, memorandum of agreements and the like, within five (5) days after perfection thereof, for review and evaluation pursuant to the afore-mentioned regulation, to protect the interest of the LGU.

Submit an explanation on the reasons for the continued non-usage of the structures and facilities.

If warranted, cause the filing of appropriate criminal and/or administrative charges against the persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Management said that negotiation is still on-going and is currently locating for these documents.
observations.reason: Documents are not yet retrieved as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to trace the material variance of ₱4,414,262.16 as of December 31, 2019 and work back on the variances in the previous year; (b) require henceforth, reconciliation of entries in the cashbook and general ledger at least on a quarterly basis and promptly resolve any significant discrepancy accordingly; and (c) instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to record the transactions in the prescribed official cashbook daily and foot and close the books at the end of each month to avoid delays in posting to the cashbook.

We recommend that Management:
Demand the immediate liquidation of unsettled accounts by the concerned employee.
Require the Municipal Accountant closely monitor unsettled cash advances of each officers and employees and send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period, a written reminder under the signature of the head of agency or his duly authorized representative, enjoining officials or employees concerned, including those that are separated from service, to immediately liquidate their cash advances.
Otherwise, require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and other claims of those officers and employees who have failed to settle their cash advances, as well as the institution of other sanctions, pursuant to Sections 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant is authorized to “delete the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash advance has been fully liquidated” as per Section 16 of Executive Order Np. 248 and Section 3.3.2(b) of COA Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal accountant to properly monitor and check payments to BIR so as to avoid double remittances. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace in the previous accounting records those balances in the “For Identification” account, in order to arrive at the correct Due to BIR account balance.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the difference between the two accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at the same account balances in the individual financial statements and eliminate these accounts in the consolidated financial statements. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to make an immediate review and conduct tracing of the differences of the reciprocal accounts once periodic financial statements are generated so as to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and provide the composition of the accounts with markings “For ID”. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify these to its appropriate account(s) and prepare the necessary working papers to validate the reliability and existence of the account balances.

We recommend that management:
be more prudent in the disbursement of funds to ensure sound fiscal administration pursuant to the provisions of Section 305 and 337 of R.A.7160; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to record and apportion correctly the share from internal revenue collections so that charges/expenditures thereof can be aptly allotted or regulated.

We recommend that management explain the recognition of obligations without the corresponding cash back-up and how these obligations will be met. Henceforth, we also recommend that management monitor cash utilization and refrain from using cash balances intended for trust funds, liabilities and reserve to avoid penalties, surcharges and legal implications in case the municipality would be unable to meet obligations.

We recommend that management submit, if there is any, the required approval/authorization from the sanggunian before the implementation of the projects. And in view of the Supreme Court’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that all appropriations shall be incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan and Project Procurement Management Plan of the LGU and implemented by the concerned department or office.

We recommend that management set aside funds for the installation of the prescribed e-NGAS both in the accounting and budget departments and henceforth, make use of the system in the submission of pertinent financial reports.

We recommend that management revise/update its Revenue Code of 2009 pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 to increase the revenue collection of the municipality so as to provide additional funds which can be used to finance beneficial programs and projects.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant record the Internal Revenue Share to General Fund – Proper only so as to determine whether enterprises managed by the Municipality is generating income sufficient to meet their respective operating costs.
observations.managementAction: Management said that the recording of the internal revenue share to the special accounts, Market and Waterworks, are on the basis that these accounts are in no way considered to be economic enterprises. There was no legislation institutionalizing them to be economic enterprises.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit justification for the incurrence of expenses that exceeded the available funds for the CY 2017 Provincial Meet. Henceforth, we recommend that management incur expenditures within the available appropriation to avoid illegal expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Management said that the negative balance was due to an error in the inclusion of a municipality’s counterpart for the activity. In effect, the counterpart for the Municipality of Trinidad was included twice when determining the total funds available for the activity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, and observe the guidelines in the identification of the programs and projects to be financed under the 20% DF in order to attain the desirable socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality.

We also recommend that management consider the realignment of the 20% Economic Development Fund continuing appropriation to fund programs and projects that would boost the socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the municipality and its constituents which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.
observations.managementAction: Management said that they will inquire to the DILG as to the propriety of the inclusion of the item in the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management indicate all the necessary data in the Purchase Orders pursuant to Section 37 of NGAS Volume II to easily determine compliance by the supplier/contractor of their responsibilities on the contract to avoid wastage of the limited funds of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions set forth in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2011-1 and 2017-1, and observe the latest guidelines in the identification of the programs and projects to be undertaken in order to attain the desirable socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality. We also recommended that Management consider the realignment of the 20% Economic Development Fund continuing appropriation to fund appropriate programs and projects which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.
observations.managementAction: Management is trying to realign projects/programs/activities already included in the 20% DF continuing appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented. We also recommended that management faithfully adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRMM report.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit immediately the necessary supporting documents required by the Office of the COA Chairman and furnish us copies of the duly received transmittal letters for the liquidation of CY 2011 and 2015 unliquidated cash advances of the present mayor, as well as those of the former mayors, if already transmitted. 

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash advances if liquidation vouchers/reports for the previous cash advances are not yet submitted to the COA Chairman, duly supported by a received copy of the transmittal letter.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the transmittal letters of the recent cash advances for confidential/intelligence fund were already forwarded to the auditor. However, previous transmittal letters are still to be located especially those of previous mayors in which the present management has no copies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’ payable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management had already reconciled some of these balances, however, the rest are still to be reconciled due to lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the contractor to submit document/s to support the claim that the delivery of HDPE Pipes from the recommended manufacturer will consume almost the entire contract duration and proof that the suspension advised by the Municipality Mayor caused delay in the excavation for the pipe laying to avoid liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a written explanation on the said delay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). 

We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: Management already sent a request for approval to BLGF and awaiting reply.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP meeting.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees.
observations.managementAction: Management wrote a letter to the VMLP with regards to this audit finding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the contractors to submit an explanation for the delay of completion and to complete the project as provided in the terms and conditions specified in the contract executed by them, if still warranted.

observations.managementAction: Management submitted a written explanation on the said delay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the MDRRMO, MSWDO and all Punong Barangays submit written justifications duly signed by all officials and personnel concerned and involved in the materials distribution, as to the non-observance of the terms of the MOA between the NHA and LGU-Sagbayan and likewise, furnish COA documents that would support their assertions as to the agreement between the constituents and Sangguniang Barangay which transpired before the actual distribution of materials.
observations.managementAction: Management already submitted some the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and web administrator or its equivalent produce and regularly update official website for the Municipality to enhance transparency and accountability on financial transactions and operations thereof pursuant to Section 98 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2015.

observations.recommendation:

Require the municipal accountant to send billings and/or demand letters to the individuals concerned who are still in the locality or whose whereabouts are known for possible collections.

If collection is futile for valid reasons, request authority from the Commission on Audit for the write off of these accounts.

Require the municipal accountant that for whatever result in the request for write off of the accounts, take the necessary adjustment in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The management has already forwarded to the local auditor of the request for write-off. For the first recommendation, manpower and financial requirements cannot be allocated for these accounts which this management believes that collection is futile. 

Taking into account the time that these accounts have been dormant, there are no more records available that this management can use to implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:

The corresponding equipment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts.  In the absence of available records of acquisitions in the past years, they will have no other recourse but to take hold of the disbursement vouchers to obtain the necessary data to be recorded in the equipment/property ledger cards.

We further recommend that management require the general services officer or municipal treasurer to submit the request for relief of accountability with complete supporting papers so that the auditors can take proper action on the request.
observations.managementAction: Management had already conducted an inventory count, however, it is not yet complete. Hence, management is going to conduct a by department inventory count in order to finish completely and will submit the inventory report after.

observations.recommendation:

Transfer the unexpended balances in accordance with Sections 98 and 99 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management said that MOAs/MOUs could not be retrieved for verification. And no claim has been made, administratively.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exhaust all possible remedies to collect these receivables, deduct the outstanding balances from the salaries or any amount due from the employees who are still connected with the municipality, if any and review, analyze and reconcile all overdue accounts to determine proper disposition of reconciled and validated accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the department heads to timely and properly plan the trainings, conventions and seminar/workshops in a year so as identify charges to the account Training Expenses. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of property, plant and equipment of the LGU. Likewise, we recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Also, we recommended that henceforth, accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of salaries or any money due to concerned employees with unliquidated cash advances and henceforth, enforce Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation thereof.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to examine the Other Payables account, prepare necessary adjustments in the books and submit to COA the corresponding copies of the adjusting entries and its supporting documents, if any, for verification.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions set forth in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2011-1 and 2017-1, and observe the latest guidelines in the identification of the programs and projects to be undertaken in order to attain the desirable socio-economic and environmental development of the municipality. We also recommended that Management consider the realignment of the 20% Economic Development Fund continuing appropriation to fund appropriate programs and projects which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

We recommended that Management transfer the said expenses to the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses of Economic Enterprise – Waterworks System and submit the journal entry voucher for its transfer. We further recommended that Management ensure that the fund is optimally used for development purposes to avoid administrative sanctions for the misuse of the 20% Development Fund beyond those prescribed by law.
We recommended that Management defer the grant of salary increase in view of the excess amount over the personal services limitation provided in Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that Management invite representatives from the Non-Government Organizations (NGOs) and duly recognized private sector representatives as observers to witness the various stages in procurement procedures, aside from representatives from COA in compliance with Section 13.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC Secretariat issue certification that the NOA was posted in conspicuous places, together with the other contract documents, to ensure observance of Section 37.1.6 of the same IRR.

We recommended that Management indicate all the necessary data in the Purchase Orders pursuant to Section 37 of NGAS Volume II to easily determine compliance by the supplier/contractor of their responsibilities on the contract to avoid wastage of the limited funds of the municipality.

We recommended that management cause the immediate submission of year-end financial reports by the Municipal Accountant. A written explanation from the Municipal Accountant explaining the delay is also recommended for submission. Henceforth, we recommended that all concerned municipal employees with pertinent data needed in the preparation and completion of financial statements, make a concerted effort in order for the Municipal Accountant to meet the legal deadline for submission of FS, in observance of COA Circular No.2010-001 dated                            March 2, 2010 and Section 122 of the P.D.1445 and to avoid imposition of penalty as stipulated in the same Section of the P.D.

We recommended that management provide an explanation on the hiring of a private lawyer who performs the functions of a legal officer. Likewise, we recommended that management observe the provisions of Sections 31 and 481 of R.A.7160 and COA Circular 98-002 on the hiring of private lawyers so as to avoid incurrence of illegal expenditures. Also, we recommended that management consider employing a legal officer, if warranted.

We recommended that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to ensure that planned program/projects/activities are implemented. We also recommended that management faithfully adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRMM report.

We recommend that Management submit immediately the necessary supporting documents required by the Office of the COA Chairman and furnish us copies of the duly received transmittal letters for the liquidation of CY 2011 and 2015 unliquidated cash advances of the present mayor, as well as those of the former mayors, if already transmitted. 

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash advances if liquidation vouchers/reports for the previous cash advances are not yet submitted to the COA Chairman, duly supported by a received copy of the transmittal letter.

Negotiate with the owner of the private lot where the Corn Post-Harvest Facilities is located for the acquisition of the said property by the LGU through sale or expropriation as mandated under Section 19 of the Local Government Code or RA 7160.

Henceforth, submit to the COA all contracts, memorandum of agreements and the like, within five (5) days after perfection thereof, for review and evaluation pursuant to the afore-mentioned regulation, to protect the interest of the LGU.

Submit an explanation on the reasons for the continued non-usage of the structures and facilities.

If warranted, cause the filing of appropriate criminal and/or administrative charges against the persons liable.
observations.reason: Documents are not yet retrieved as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: Management should exercise the taxing and revenue raising powers of the LGU by enforcing payment of taxes and fees and impose sanctions and penalties on delinquent water consumers uniformly without any discrimination and favor pursuant to Section 130 of RA 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepares the journal entries
in order to adjust the cash in bank of the LGU with proper substantiation for each
reconciling item.

It is further recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statements of all Cash
in Bank accounts of the municipality be prepared and submitted on time, as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-011. The failure to submit required reports may
warrant the withholding of the salary of the concerned official.

We recommend that Management submit immediately the necessary
supporting documents required by the Office of the COA Chairman and furnish us
copies of the duly received transmittal letters for the liquidation of CY 2011 and
2015 unliquidated cash advances of the present mayor, as well as those of the former
mayors, if already transmitted.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant refrain from granting
additional cash advances if liquidation vouchers/reports for the previous cash
advances are not yet submitted to the COA Chairman, duly supported by a received
copy of the transmittal letter.

We recommend that Management demand from the officers and employees
concerned for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash
advances through issuance of demand letters issued by the Municipal Accountant
and noted by the Local Chief Executive, copy furnished the Audit Team. In case of
non-compliance, direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the payment of their
salaries or any money due to them.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory
Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the property, plant and
equipment of the LGU and prepare the inventory reports and submit a copy thereof
to the Auditor in compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect adjustments of
the discrepancies between the actual and recorded balances of the affected accounts.

In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we
recommend that the local government unit return the unutilized PDAF balance to
the Bureau of Treasury for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the general
fund of the national government in accordance with the Supreme Court decision
dated November 19, 2013.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
ascertain whether or not these payable accounts are still due for remittance to
concerned offices. If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest
of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’
payable accounts.

We recommend that Management require the contractor to submit
document/s to support the claim that the delivery of HDPE Pipes from the
recommended manufacturer will consume almost the entire contract duration and
proof that the suspension advised by the Municipality Mayor caused delay in the
excavation for the pipe laying to avoid liquidated damages.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to
transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing
bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).

We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau
of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not
compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department
Circular 001-2015.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend the Management to ensure that the fund is optimally used
for development purposes to avoid administrative sanctions for the misuse of the
20% Development Fund beyond those prescribed by law.

We recommend that management submit justification in writing on why
financial assistance under the AICS Program were granted to clients despite the
absence of supporting documents as enumerated in Section 4.2 of DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 15, Series of 2014.

Also, direct the Municipal Social Welfare Officer to submit the records of the
cases and all other basic supporting documents relative to the claims for further
evaluation, otherwise the transactions will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary
adjustments on the above collections to its appropriate fund accounts.
Consequently, we recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant adhere to
the proper recording of collections to ensure that the financial statements of the
municipality are presented fairly.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the proper and
correct classification of accounts. Preparation of journal entries as well as financial
statements shall always be in accordance with the Chart of Accounts as prescribed
in COA Circular No. 2015-009, to be able to maintain accurate and reliable
accounting records, and to ensure that the financial statements of the municipality
are presented fairly.

We recommend that the officials and employees of the municipality adhere to
Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the
grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the
Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top
of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the
activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning
local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.

We recommend that management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the
basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts
contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP
meeting.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer not to accept the old receipts of the
leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National
Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and
registration fees.

We recommend that the management, in coordination with the Local School
Board, revisit the provisions of R.A.5477 in order to arrive at identification and
implementation of projects/activities which are more fruitful and reflective of the
intents of R.A.5477, to fully serve its purpose.

We recommend that management prepare the Solid Waste Management
Plan of the municipality to comply with the requirements of the Ecological Solid
Waste Management Act of 2000 and implement the same.

We recommend the Municipal Health Officer to check the drugs and
medicines to be included in the Project Procurement Management Plan whether
these are included and conform to the Essential Drug List of the Philippine National
Drug Formulatory.

We recommend the Municipal Health Officer and the Municipal Budget
Officer to submit justification/s for charging these expenses to Philhealth Capitation
Fund.

We recommend that Management submit copies of the perfected government
contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents within five (5) working
days from execution/issuance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to
facilitate timely auditorial and technical review of contracts and purchase orders
and to avoid imposition of administrative disciplinary action prescribed by laws.

We recommend that management and/or officials responsible for or in
charge of accepting deliveries of procured items regardless of amount shall, within
twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance, furnish the Auditor with copies of the
delivery receipts and inspection and acceptance report and notify the said Auditor
of the deliveries in order to enable to prepare for and perform timely inspection of
the same.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and RFEUS, provide
explanation in the giving of CNA despite the absence of a valid and subsisting CNA.
